verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Timothy, Titus, and Philemon.
verses.text: The remark of Photius, that St. Chrysostom’s more finished works were those which he composed at Antioch, does not seem to afford a sufficient criterion for assigning a date to each set of Homilies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Timothy, Titus, and Philemon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Timothy, Titus, and Philemon.
verses.text: One can hardly doubt the allusion, in reading the full account in the Homily on St. Babylas; nor can it well be supposed that he would thus refer to it as a thing well known at any other place than Antioch.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Timothy, Titus, and Philemon.
verses.text: The Homilies on the Epistle to Titus are fixed at Antioch by the mention of Daphne and the cave of Matrona in Hom. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Timothy, Titus, and Philemon.
verses.text: The composition of these Homilies has been remarked on as negligent by Hemsterhusius, so that he takes them to have been extemporaneous effusions taken down by others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Timothy, Titus, and Philemon.
verses.text: There may be some ground for this in the style, and in the paraphrastic character of the various readings, but as a commentary they are unusually closed and exact, and point out much of what regards the persuasive character of the Epistle that is not generally noticed.
